channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Vibe coding upstart Lovable denies data leak, cites 'intentional behavior,' then throws HackerOne under the bus
item.description: Lovable, a vibe-coding platform, denies a data leak reported by a researcher, suggesting it was due to 'intentional behavior' and unclear documentation, while also blaming HackerOne.

item.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
item.description: Amazon invests $5 billion in Anthropic, which in return agrees to spend $100 billion on AWS, reinforcing the cloud provider's dominance in AI.

item.title: Trump-branded datacenter project fails to make itself great, again
item.description: Fermi America, a datacenter project, faces challenges in its goal to expand to 17 GW capacity, reflecting ongoing struggles in the datacenter industry.

item.title: PrivacyBee review: An Incogni alternative that made data removal feel nearly effortless
item.description: PrivacyBee offers a comprehensive data removal service that simplifies the process of erasing personal information from various websites.

item.title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
item.description: An autonomous robot from Honor set a new half-marathon record in China, completing the race in 50:26, surpassing the human record.

item.title: World's blandest man steps down from CEO job to spend more time in tastefully appointed home
item.description: Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO of Apple after 15 years, transitioning to executive chairman, with John Ternus taking over leadership.

item.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in 7 new countries
item.description: Google has launched its Gemini feature in Chrome across seven new countries, enhancing user experience on both desktop and iOS.

item.title: Chase got a spiff of $77 million to create one job with New York datacenter
item.description: JPMorgan Chase received $77 million in tax breaks for a New York datacenter project that is expected to create only one permanent job, raising concerns about economic benefits.

item.title: I speed-tested Surfshark's new 'heavenly' Dausos VPN protocol - how it compares to WireGuard
item.description: Surfshark's new Dausos VPN protocol promises enhanced security and speed, competing with existing protocols like WireGuard.

item.title: Claude Desktop changes app access settings for browsers you don't even have installed yet
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Desktop for macOS has been criticized for modifying app settings without user consent, raising potential legal issues under EU law.

item.title: Accelerate Generative AI Inference on Amazon SageMaker AI with G7e Instances
item.description: Amazon SageMaker AI has launched G7e instances featuring NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 GPUs, enhancing generative AI inference capabilities. This allows users to host powerful open-source models efficiently.

item.title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing
item.description: A common phrase structure in AI-generated writing has become a strong indicator of synthetic content, making it easier to identify such texts. This trend highlights the growing sophistication of AI writing.

item.title: How to easily encrypt files on an Android phone - and the free app I use to do it
item.description: An article provides a simple guide on encrypting files on Android devices using a free app, ensuring secure sharing of sensitive information. This is essential for privacy-conscious users.

item.title: Linux 7.1 will have an optional new NTFS driver
item.description: Linux kernel 7.1 will introduce an optional new NTFS driver, allowing read-write access to NTFS file systems. This is a significant improvement for users needing compatibility with Windows.

item.title: Changes to GitHub Copilot Individual plans
item.description: GitHub is updating its Copilot Individual plans to provide a more reliable experience for users. These changes aim to enhance service predictability and customer satisfaction.

item.title: Google Pixel phones have a useful voicemail feature that's hidden by default - how to enable it
item.description: Google Pixel phones feature a hidden 'Take A Message' voicemail tool that helps manage calls when users are unavailable. This feature enhances user convenience.

item.title: Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals
item.description: GitHub is making changes to its Copilot plans for individuals to ensure service reliability. This is part of ongoing efforts to enhance user experience.

item.title: Silicon Valley has forgotten what normal people want
item.description: The tech industry often overlooks the needs of everyday users, as evidenced by a conversation about LLMs that highlighted a disconnect between tech enthusiasts and general consumer desires.

item.title: I tried to wipe my digital footprint without paying for a data removal service - 5 free ways
item.description: An article outlines five free methods for individuals to remove their personal data from the internet, emphasizing the importance of digital privacy and control over personal information.

item.title: I've tested every major phone release in 2026 so far - and my buying advice is changing this year
item.description: A review of major phone releases in 2026 reveals a growing appreciation for last year's models, indicating shifts in consumer preferences and expectations in smartphone technology.

item.title: UK.gov kicks off half-a-billion quid sovereign AI venture with £80M invite
item.description: The UK government is initiating £80 million in AI procurement discussions as part of a £500 million sovereign capability fund, allowing companies to retain intellectual property from government projects. This move aims to bolster the UK's AI capabilities and innovation.

item.title: The Download: murderous ‘mirror’ bacteria, and Chinese workers fighting AI doubles
item.description: This edition of The Download covers various tech topics, including concerns about synthetic mirror bacteria and the impact of AI on Chinese workers. It highlights the ongoing debates and developments in technology and its implications for society.

item.title: What Do Your Logits Know? (The Answer May Surprise You!)
item.description: Recent research reveals that probing the internals of AI models can expose sensitive information, posing risks of data leakage. This study emphasizes the need for careful management of AI model outputs to prevent unintentional information exposure.

item.title: Nigel Farage vows to deport 400,000 to end 'invasion of Britain'
item.description: Nigel Farage has announced a controversial plan to deport 400,000 asylum seekers in the UK, framing it as a response to an 'invasion'. This pledge is central to his party's campaign and is expected to ignite significant political debate.

item.title: claude-ctx 0.6.0
item.description: The release of Claude's new version 0.6.0 introduces a skill and agent recommendation system, enhancing its capabilities with knowledge graphs and quality control features. This update aims to improve user experience and functionality.

item.title: Argentines Turn To Donkey Meat Amid Javier Milei's Economic Crisis
item.description: Amid economic turmoil under President Javier Milei, Argentines are turning to donkey meat as a cheaper alternative to beef, which has become prohibitively expensive. This shift reflects the broader economic challenges facing the country.

item.title: Cloud deployment firm Vercel breached, advises secrets rotation
item.description: Vercel, a cloud deployment firm, has reported a security breach linked to a small third-party AI tool, prompting the company to advise users to rotate their secrets. This incident raises concerns about third-party integrations in tech.

item.title: cdx-manager 0.4.4
item.description: The release of cdx-manager version 0.4.4 introduces a terminal session manager for Codex and Claude accounts, aiming to streamline user interactions and improve efficiency in managing sessions.

item.title: 株式会社StartLinkは、HubSpot Solutions PartnerプログラムでGoldティアのSolutions Partnerに昇格しました。
item.description: StartLink has achieved Gold Tier status in the HubSpot Solutions Partner program, indicating a high level of expertise and capability in providing HubSpot solutions. This recognition can enhance their market credibility.

item.title: Arixify – Insert prompts into AI chats with one click, no retyping needed
item.description: Arixify has launched a tool that allows users to insert prompts into AI chats with a single click, eliminating the need for retyping. This innovation aims to improve user efficiency in interacting with AI.

item.title: Samsung SmartThings Expands Seamless Integration With IKEA Matter Devices
item.description: Samsung's SmartThings platform has improved integration with IKEA's smart home products, allowing users to connect 25 Matter-over-Thread devices seamlessly. This enhances the functionality of smart homes by broadening device compatibility.

item.title: Apple Testing Two Small Yet Useful iOS 27 Options
item.description: Apple is testing new 'Undo' and 'Redo' features for Home Screen customization in iOS 27, making it easier for users to modify their layouts. This update aims to enhance user experience by simplifying the customization process.

item.title: unslop added to PyPI
item.description: The unslop package has been added to PyPI, designed to humanize markdown and text files by removing AI-related language while keeping essential content intact. This tool is useful for developers looking to refine documentation.

item.title: modelito 1.0.1
item.description: Modelito 1.0.1 offers lightweight Python abstractions and connectors for various LLM providers, including OpenAI and Claude. This simplifies the integration process for developers working with different AI models.

item.title: hol-guard 2.0.32
item.description: HOL Guard 2.0.32 has been released to protect local AI harnesses before executing tools in various AI environments. This update enhances security measures for AI applications.

item.title: Plans emerge for 1 gigawatt AI data centre in the Kimberley
item.description: Plans for a massive 1 gigawatt AI data center in Western Australia's Kimberley region have emerged, aimed at training models like ChatGPT. This project represents a significant investment in AI infrastructure.

item.title: astrolab-cli 1.0.4
item.description: AstroLab CLI 1.0.4 is a new tool that utilizes real NASA data and Google Gemini AI for space-themed studies. This tool aims to enhance educational and research opportunities in space science.

item.title: hol-guard 2.0.31
item.description: HOL Guard 2.0.31, similar to version 2.0.32, also focuses on protecting local AI harnesses before executing tools in various AI environments. This emphasizes ongoing improvements in AI security.

item.title: neuroreg 0.2.0.dev0
item.description: Neuroreg 0.2.0.dev0 is a new package for 3D neuroimaging registration using PyTorch, aimed at researchers in neuroscience. This tool facilitates advanced imaging analysis.

item.title: rasptorch 3.4.0
item.description: Rasptorch 3.4.0 introduces an experimental autograd engine similar to PyTorch, with a Vulkan compute backend tailored for Raspberry Pi 5. This aims to enhance performance for edge computing applications.

item.title: 5 Defining Dramatic Tropes That Started With Shakespeare
item.description: The article explores five key dramatic tropes introduced by Shakespeare, such as mistaken identity and tragic heroes, that continue to influence modern storytelling.

item.title: WWDC 2026 Graphic Teases Major iOS 27 Feature
item.description: Apple's WWDC 2026 graphic hints at a new Siri interface for iOS 27, integrating it into the Dynamic Island feature, as reported by Bloomberg's Mark Gurman.

item.title: astrolab-cli 1.0.3
item.description: AstroLab is a new command-line interface tool that utilizes real NASA data and Google Gemini AI, aimed at space-themed studies.

item.title: Show HN: Agentjail – Self Hosted Freestyle.sh
item.description: Agentjail is a minimal Linux sandbox designed for executing untrusted code, particularly useful for AI agents and build systems.

item.title: Google And Gucci Collaborate On Luxury AI-Enhanced Smart Glasses For 2027
item.description: Google is collaborating with Gucci to create luxury AI-enhanced smart glasses, aiming to transform smart eyewear into a fashionable accessory by 2027.

item.title: AI 'agent' fever comes with lurking security threats
item.description: The rise of AI agents, like OpenClaw, promises efficiency but raises security concerns among experts due to their increasing capabilities.

item.title: OpenAI’s existential questions | TechCrunch
item.description: The latest episode of Equity discusses OpenAI's recent acquisitions and the existential challenges they aim to address for the company.

item.title: gh-sops 1.0.1
item.description: gh-sops is a new command-line interface tool that generates standard operating procedures using the GitHub Copilot SDK.

item.title: voidly-pay 1.0.1
item.description: Voidly Pay is a Python SDK that facilitates off-chain transactions and hiring for AI agents, integrating with platforms like LangChain and CrewAI.

item.title: gh-sops added to PyPI
item.description: gh-sops has been added to PyPI, providing a CLI-driven SOP generator powered by the GitHub Copilot SDK.
